Alternatively, based on some online searches, I can suggest that you may be referring to Uncaria or Uncaria Tomentosa, which is also known as Uragoga stellaris by some sources. 

This plant is a woody climber native to the tropical forests of South America. The health benefits of Uncaria Tomentosa include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimalarial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions.

The bioactive constituents of Uncaria Tomentosa include alkaloids, such as isoveratropine and hirsutine, as well as other compounds like quinovic acid glycosides.

The most common herbal preparations made with Uncaria Tomentosa include teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory disorders.
